note: Researched by Gemini (Google Search grounding), then fact-checked and link-corrected by Claude before publishing.

items.title: Anthropic releases Claude Opus 4.8
items.summary: Anthropic's most capable model yet — sharper judgment, catches its own mistakes, and a new 'dynamic workflows' mode for very large tasks.

items.title: Meta adds 'AI Mode' search to Facebook
items.summary: Facebook's search now answers questions in plain language from public posts, Groups and Reels — instead of just showing a list of links.

items.title: Gemma 4 12B runs real AI on your laptop
items.summary: Google's new open model understands text, images and audio together — and runs offline on a 16GB laptop, no internet needed.

items.title: ChatGPT gets a 'Dreaming' memory
items.summary: OpenAI's new memory learns from your past chats in the background to stay relevant over time — no more manually saving things.

items.title: GLM-5.2: a top open coding model from China
items.summary: Zhipu AI open-sourced (MIT) a 744B-parameter, 1M-token-context model built for coding that rivals leading closed models.

items.title: Moonshot AI releases Kimi K2.7 Code
items.summary: A 1-trillion-parameter open-weight model tuned for coding and multi-step agent tasks, with full weights published free on Hugging Face.

items.title: Microsoft's MAI-Code-1-Flash lands in GitHub Copilot
items.summary: A fast, small coding model now built into Copilot for everyday developer help — available across all Copilot plans, starting in VS Code.

items.title: DeepMind maps the path from AGI to superintelligence
items.summary: A 57-page paper lays out four possible routes from human-level AI to systems that could outperform whole teams of experts.

items.title: OpenAI's LifeSciBench tests AI on real science
items.summary: A 750-task benchmark built with 173 scientists. Even the best AI passes only ~36% — a reality check on how far research AI still has to go.
